fingerprint: Condition 1 of the Evaluation Guide cites 'disc radius 20,015 km' alongside H(r) and κ as one of 'the model's own stated parameters' from which a discriminating prediction should be derivable — but 20,015 km = π × R_earth to within 0.0007%. Any prediction derived using this parameter necessarily imports globe geometry; the EG implicitly concedes that 20,015 km is an independent dome input when it is actually the globe's half-circumference written on a disc edge.
location: docs/index.html — §eg-falsifiability, Condition 1 (line ~454 of current build): 'Use the model\'s own stated parameters — H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619), disc radius 20,015 km, κ = 1.67 nT/μGal — to derive a quantitative prediction that differs from what standard physics predicts...'
globe_value: π × R_earth = π × 6,371 km = 20,015.09 km (to the full precision of the globe's mean radius)
dome_value: a = 20,015 km (dome disc semi-major axis / ice-wall radius, cited in EG Condition 1)
why_it_matters: Condition 1 is the headline falsification escape hatch we offer the dome author: 'derive a discriminating prediction from your own parameters and we will upgrade the verdict.' The three parameters listed — H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619), disc radius 20,015 km, κ = 1.67 nT/μGal — are framed as independent dome inputs. Two of the three are locally-fitted constants (H coefficients, κ) with acknowledged problems documented elsewhere in the review. The third, a = 20,015 km, is not an independent parameter at all: it is the azimuthal-equidistant projection of a sphere of radius R_earth, with the antipodal south pole at distance π × R_earth from the pole. A dome defender who obliges Condition 1 by 'deriving something from disc radius 20,015 km' is in fact deriving from globe circumference re-labelled. The EG correctly flags the 6,371 km smuggle in Condition 3 (Schumann) and the WGS84 smuggle in Condition 4 (coordinates). Condition 1 is the third sibling in that family and currently silent on the same point.
why_this_is_not_duplicative: EXEC-SUMMARY.json already flags a = 20,015 km ≈ πR (along with b ≈ 15,000 km and r_equator ≈ 10,008 km) in the Executive Summary / §1.1. SEC-2.1, SEC-2.9, SEC-4.9, WIN-001, WIN-002 each flag variants of the same identity in their local contexts. The EVAL-GUIDE fingerprint is a distinct rhetorical site: it appears in the normative falsification criteria we offer the dome author as 'what would change our verdict.' Inadvertently conceding that 20,015 km is a genuine dome parameter there weakens the offer — it implies a dome defender can use this parameter without restriction. The EG should either (a) note the globe origin inline with the parameter list, or (b) exclude 20,015 km from the list of 'dome's own stated parameters' on the grounds that it is an azimuthal-equidistant projection identity, not an independent input.
kernel_acknowledged: The dome author did not invent 20,015 km arbitrarily — he adopted the Gleason/azimuthal-equidistant projection circle radius, which is πR_earth. Within the dome framework this is treated as the 'natural' disc edge (the Antarctic ice wall). The kernel of truth: the dome author correctly identified that the azimuthal-equidistant projection has the equator at πR/2 and the antipode at πR, and that these are the natural scale markers on a flat polar-azimuthal map. This is mathematically coherent cartography. The problem is that the dome author then promotes these projection markers to 'independent dome geometry parameters,' obscuring the globe that generates them. Our Evaluation Guide reproduces this promotion by citing a = 20,015 km without the inline annotation.

suggested_text_patch.where: docs/index.html §eg-falsifiability, Condition 1 (currently in sections.json as part of the evaluation_guide / eg-falsifiability prose)
suggested_text_patch.current_text: Use the model's own stated parameters — H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619), disc radius 20,015 km, κ = 1.67 nT/μGal — to derive a quantitative prediction that differs from what standard physics predicts for the same observable.
suggested_text_patch.proposed_text: Use the model's own stated parameters — H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619), disc radius 20,015 km (= π × R_earth to 7 ppm; see Section 2.1 on why this disc radius is the azimuthal-equidistant projection of the globe rather than an independent dome input), κ = 1.67 nT/μGal — to derive a quantitative prediction that differs from what standard physics predicts for the same observable. A 'discriminating prediction' that depends on a parameter already equal to πR_earth is not in fact independent of globe geometry.
suggested_text_patch.rationale: Aligns Condition 1 with Conditions 3 and 4, which already flag globe inputs (6,371 km in the Schumann challenge; WGS84 in the coordinate challenge). Cross-links to Section 2.1 where the identity is argued in full. Preempts the dome-defender move 'I derived X from dome parameters' — by denying that 20,015 km is an uncontested dome parameter.

code_search.found_in: The 20,015 km literal appears in inject_ai_layer.py as DISC_RADIUS_KM = 20015 (hardcoded), in raw-text/05-model.txt as the elliptical semi-major axis, and in monitor.py as the r-bound for gravity / refraction checks. No script in the dome repo computes 20,015 from π × R_earth — the value is imported whole. From our side, docs/index.html references 20,015 km in ~20 places; EG Condition 1 is the only normative-falsification context where it appears without a globe-origin annotation.
code_search.notes: The dome treats 20,015 km as a datum. It is not derived in the dome repo from any sub-parameter. Our review treats 20,015 km as a dome parameter in the EG Condition 1 list without flagging that it equals πR_earth. The fingerprint is visible by inspection: 20,015 / π = 6,371.03 km, i.e. Earth's standard mean radius.

follow_up_suggestions: Decider: integrate suggested_text_patch into sections.json (evaluation_guide block, eg-falsifiability Condition 1).

Also consider a lighter-touch alternative: replace 'disc radius 20,015 km' in Condition 1 with 'H(r) fit coefficients' alone, and move the 20,015 = πR discussion to Section 2.1 (where it already lives) with a cross-link. This avoids rewriting Condition 1 around a fingerprint critique.

Curmudgeon c3 review of EVAL-GUIDE should check whether the falsification criteria remain falsifiable after the patch — the patch strengthens the review but must not collapse the challenge into unfalsifiability.
